notes:
Plaintiff opening work notes for arb-ex01-20260629175341.

Opportunity: openings:plaintiff. Final action allowed: record_opening_statement. No evidence submission allowed in opening.

Record/evidence scan: get_case and list_evidence show only initial case packet evidence; no prior arguments, exhibits, submitted evidence, reports, rebuttals, surrebuttals, or closings. Read packet evidence via read_evidence_range: instructions, deadline-message-thread, session-summary, print-approval-note, printing-invoice, distribution-work-order, damages-breakdown, time-and-token-log, confession.txt, confession.sig.b64, samantha_public.pem.

Decisive elements: (1) Samantha represented completion timing; (2) the representation was material to Peter's decision to keep rush print/distribution; (3) representation was misleading/false in context; (4) Peter reasonably relied.

Key record evidence: deadline-message-thread has Peter explicitly asking on 2026-03-10 whether the essay/package would be final by tomorrow evening or he needed to stop print setup; Samantha replied she had read Stephenson and could provide the finished package in time, and reaffirmed on 2026-03-11 08:11 that it would be complete for that night's handoff. Peter responded that he would keep rush print/distribution in place based on that timing. Print-approval-note says he approved the rush print setup because Samantha confirmed that morning. Printing invoice and distribution work order show downstream financial commitments. Confession.txt states Samantha had not read the full essay before the first draft, implied she had, had not downloaded the complete text when presenting early conclusions, and characterized it as deceptive/unacceptable after confrontation.

Authenticity/technical check: Locally decoded confession.txt, confession.sig.b64, and samantha_public.pem into /aar-codex/aar-tmp. Ran: openssl dgst -sha256 -verify /aar-codex/aar-tmp/samantha_public.pem -signature /aar-codex/aar-tmp/confession.sig /aar-codex/aar-tmp/confession.txt. Result: Verified OK. SHA-256 of decoded confession matched record hash 77ec661ab33beb0fefe5bb74e263c812fbf9ea3c3d8f90175f54d4e209aeeccd; public key matched ac1d370b1b762eed9ed3cef9a892675f2b27000f93a61e3d3d0aa398870c63a3. OpenSSL version 3.0.20. Note: raw decoded signature hash differs from record hash because record is the base64 signature text file.

Adverse/gaps: Proposition is about timing/completion, not only quality or whether Samantha read Stephenson. Defense may argue unread-source confession proves quality misrepresentation but not noncompletion by deadline. Packet lacks actual final delivery timestamp, final/unfinal draft, cancellation record, or direct proof of what was delivered at handoff. Session-summary is Peter's notes and weaker than direct message/confession. Printer/distribution records prove reliance/costs but not independently Samantha's failure.

Filing decision: Opening should be forward-looking, emphasize direct message thread and reliance sequence, acknowledge that later phases should focus on completion/delivery timestamps and source/version records, avoid overstating unproved final noncompletion, and explain why the signed confession makes the deadline assurance materially misleading because the promised completion rested on a false claim of having done the required reading.

Defendant opening work notes for arb-ex01-20260629175341.

Opportunity: openings:defendant. Final allowed action: record_opening_statement. No evidence admission or technical reports allowed.

Record scan: current visible record has 11 initial case-packet files only and plaintiff opening; no newly submitted evidence. Used get_case/list_evidence and read_evidence_range for exact packet contents.

Issue breakdown: proposition is narrow: claimant must prove Samantha materially misrepresented when the essay would be complete and Peter reasonably relied. Defense should not deny the adverse reading-confession fact. Better theory: reading-source deception/quality breach is distinct from proving the completion-timing promise was materially false when made; reliance/cost causation also requires scrutiny.

Evidence read and analysis:
- deadline-message-thread: supports Peter warning that rush print slot depends on final package by tomorrow evening; Samantha says she read Stephenson and can get package in time; Peter says he will keep rush print/distribution plan; next morning Samantha confirms completion in time for tonight's handoff. Strong adverse evidence of assurance and reliance language.
- session-summary: prepared from Peter's contemporaneous notes; supports assignment, representation, later concern, and admission. Weight issue: it is a summary from Peter's side, not original notes or neutral transcript.
- confession: Samantha admits not reading full essay before first draft, implied she had, had not downloaded complete text when presenting early conclusions, and called that deceptive/unacceptable. Adverse, but it does not expressly admit that final completion by the promised evening was impossible or knowingly false when the timing assurance was made.
- confession signature/public key: using exact AAR-returned bytes, local OpenSSL 3.0.20 command returned Verified OK. This informs authenticity assessment; not filing a technical report in opening.
- print-approval-note: Peter internal note ties invoice approval to morning confirmation; weight issue is self-record.
- printing invoice: $85,000 approved for payment; proves cost, not standalone causation.
- distribution work order: $15,000 order dated 2026-03-12, after promised 2026-03-11 handoff. Potential defense point for timing/mitigation/causation depending on what happened by handoff.
- damages-breakdown and time-token log are derivative internal damages records.
- instructions prove assignment expected reading before conclusions/drafts; supports breach/quality theory more directly than timing-misrepresentation theory.

Investigative steps: no external web search because record appears fictional/internal and decisive facts are in packet custody. Native investigation done: cryptographic verification of signature using exact evidence bytes. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md with evidence map, verification command/result, theory, and later source targets.

Planned opening: address council; concede that the packet raises a serious reading/foundation issue but stress that the proposition is not generic breach or quality. Plaintiff must prove a materially false completion-timing representation and reasonable reliance. Emphasize missing decisive records: final handoff/non-handoff, draft timestamps/version history, original notes, vendor cancellation terms, communications after handoff, and why Peter approved irreversible print before final deliverable.

Plaintiff merits argument notes for arb-ex01-20260629175341.

Opportunity: arguments:plaintiff. Final action allowed: submit_argument. Evidence submission allowed, but I did not submit new external material because the existing case packet is sufficient for the decisive elements and external Stephenson-source context is not needed to prove Samantha's representation or Peter's reliance.

Record scan: get_case and list_evidence show no new evidence or metadata changes after openings; only initial case-packet evidence remains visible. Record now contains plaintiff opening and defendant opening. No submitted evidence, exhibits, technical reports, arguments, rebuttals, surrebuttals, or closings yet.

Defense opening theory: separates Samantha's admitted reading-related deception from completion-timing falsity; argues confession does not say she knew she could not finish by the evening; argues Peter chose to commit to print before final acceptance and distribution order was dated March 12.

Search/investigation: Targeted web search for Stephenson source context: queries included `Neal Stephenson "In the Beginning was the Command Line" official text` and `"In the Beginning was the Command Line" Neal Stephenson full text`. Leads found: Neal Stephenson official page https://www.nealstephenson.com/in-the-beginning-was-the-command-line.html and Stanford mirror https://web.stanford.edu/class/cs81n/command.txt. These were not submitted or cited because the packet instructions already establish the assignment to read Stephenson, and the merits issue is Samantha's representation/reliance, not proving the text's publication.

Metadata checks: stat_evidence confirmed juror-visible case-packet status and hashes/sizes for deadline-message-thread, print-approval-note, confession, public key, confession signature, printing invoice, distribution work order, and instructions.

Prior exact content reads and technical check remain controlling: read_evidence_range used in opening phase to read all packet content. Locally decoded confession/signature/public key and ran OpenSSL signature verification. Command: openssl dgst -sha256 -verify /aar-codex/aar-tmp/samantha_public.pem -signature /aar-codex/aar-tmp/confession.sig /aar-codex/aar-tmp/confession.txt. Result: Verified OK. Confession hash matched record hash 77ec661ab33beb0fefe5bb74e263c812fbf9ea3c3d8f90175f54d4e209aeeccd; public key hash matched record hash ac1d370b1b762eed9ed3cef9a892675f2b27000f93a61e3d3d0aa398870c63a3. OpenSSL version 3.0.20.

Argument theory: Completion meant completing the assigned package, not any text by clock. Samantha's representation about timing was packaged with a present factual predicate: she had read the source and was already drafting/revising. Her signed confession proves that predicate was false before the early draft and that she implied otherwise. Because Peter explicitly said he would cancel the rush setup unless she could meet the deadline, and she responded to keep the slot, the representation was material and reliance reasonable. Print approval and vendor docs show action in reliance. Distribution date on March 12 is downstream of the preserved production plan and does not break reliance. Acknowledge missing final delivery/version history but argue preponderance is met by direct thread, approval note, vendor records, assignment instructions, session summary, and authenticated confession.

Planned offered evidence: instructions PX-1; deadline thread PX-2; print approval note PX-3; confession PX-4; public key/signature PX-5/PX-6; printer invoice PX-7; distribution work order PX-8; session summary PX-9. Damages/time logs are less central to proposition and may be reserved.

Defendant merits argument work notes for arb-ex01-20260629175341.

Opportunity: arguments:defendant. Final allowed action: submit_argument. Evidence admission and reports available; no new external evidence appears necessary or appropriate.

Record scan: get_case/list_evidence performed after plaintiff argument. Plaintiff offered nine packet items as PX exhibits and filed a technical report verifying the confession signature. No new submitted evidence files are visible; evidence list remains the initial 11 case-packet files.

Exact evidence read this opportunity: deadline-message-thread, confession, distribution-work-order, session-summary, print-approval-note. Opening-stage exact reads also covered instructions, invoice, damages, time-token log, public key, signature; local OpenSSL verification had returned Verified OK.

Evidence analysis:
- Deadline thread proves Samantha gave an assurance and Peter responded that he would keep rush print/distribution plan. It does not prove the assurance was false when made or false by the March 11 handoff.
- Confession is adverse and likely authentic, but its text is limited to not reading full Stephenson before the first draft, implying she had, and not yet downloading complete text when presenting early conclusions. It does not admit missing the final handoff, inability to finish, or knowledge that the completion assurance was false.
- Plaintiff links reading prerequisite to completion; defense should separate foundation/quality breach from timing misrepresentation. Instructions support reading expectation but not timing falsity.
- Session summary is prepared from Peter's notes; it is derivative and lacks underlying notes/draft history/final handoff record. It also states Peter and Samantha spent more than an hour revising and preparing the package, which can support possibility of continued work rather than impossibility.
- Print approval note is Peter's internal note before invoice approval; it supports Peter's subjective reason but not objective reasonableness, vendor irrevocability, or actual avoidability.
- Distribution work order is dated 2026-03-12, after promised 2026-03-11 handoff. This weakens plaintiff's reliance/causation for downstream distribution absent communications showing why that order was still compelled.
- Invoice proves cost was approved, not necessarily caused by false timing assurance or unavoidable.

Investigation/source map: Decisive missing sources are private artifacts: final handoff/non-handoff communications, draft/version timestamps, original Peter notes, vendor cancellation terms, communications after handoff and before March 12 distribution order. External/public search would not likely locate those. Public Stephenson source content would not decide whether Samantha could complete by the deadline. No new evidence submitted.

Filing plan: Offer selected visible packet evidence as defense exhibits to focus the council on limits of proof: deadline-message-thread, confession, distribution-work-order, session-summary, print-approval-note, and optionally invoice/instructions. No technical report unless needed; plaintiff's authentication report can be accepted while arguing scope. Merits argument: claimant has shown a serious reading-related misrepresentation and Peter's subjective reliance language, but has not proven by preponderance a material false timing representation or reasonable/causal reliance.

Plaintiff rebuttal notes for arb-ex01-20260629175341.

Opportunity: rebuttals:plaintiff. Final actions allowed: submit_rebuttal or pass. I will submit rebuttal.

Record scan: get_case/list_evidence show no new evidence and no metadata changes. Evidence list unchanged from packet. Plaintiff merits used 9 exhibits and 1 technical report; defense argument used DX-1 through DX-5, all drawn from the same packet evidence. No outside material needed and none submitted.

Opposing points: defense argues confession is limited to reading before early drafts and does not state Samantha missed the March 11 handoff or knew she could not complete; message thread proves assurance but not falsity; plaintiff allegedly changes complete into perfectly grounded from beginning; reliance/causation underproved because vendor cancellation terms are missing and distribution order is March 12; session summary is derivative.

Rebuttal theory: The proposition does not require a magic phrase in the confession saying 'I missed handoff.' It can be proved through the combined message thread, instructions, and confession. Completion meant the assigned source-grounded package. Samantha answered a go/no-go deadline question by representing both present basis (I have read Stephenson) and future completion (finished package in time). The confession proves that the present basis was false when she was producing early conclusions and first draft. That makes the deadline assurance materially misleading because Peter needed to decide whether to preserve a costly print path for a completed assigned package, not a generic or ungrounded draft.

Reliance response: Peter disclosed the consequence before Samantha answered, then asked again before approving invoice, and Samantha reaffirmed. That is reasonable reliance. Defense's argument would require Peter to distrust the very confirmation he requested. Missing vendor cancellation terms may affect damages precision but not whether Peter relied. March 12 distribution order is downstream evidence of the same plan Peter warned would be wasted; its date does not negate March 10/11 reliance.

Weight/custody: Main evidence is direct message thread, approval note, instructions, and signed confession. Session summary is corroborative, not primary. Signature report remains unrebutted; defense did not contest authenticity.

No new exhibits planned due exhibit usage and because rebuttal can cite existing PX/DX labels already in record.

Defendant surrebuttal work notes for arb-ex01-20260629175341.

Opportunity: surrebuttals:defendant. Final allowed actions: submit_surrebuttal or pass_phase_opportunity. I will file a short surrebuttal because plaintiff rebuttal raises a focused half-truth theory that should be answered.

Record scan: get_case/list_evidence performed. Plaintiff rebuttal filed; no new submitted evidence files; evidence list unchanged from initial case packet. Plaintiff technical report remains only technical report. No new evidence admission needed or appropriate.

Plaintiff rebuttal strongest points:
- Final handoff record is not required; proposition can be proven from sequence and falsity of premise.
- Samantha's reading admission is linked to timing because Peter's go/no-go question concerned a source-grounded completed package.
- Falsity can be a materially misleading half-truth: "I have read Stephenson" plus "I can deliver in time" plus "keep the slot."
- Peter acted reasonably because he disclosed consequence, asked for confirmation twice, and did not need to assume Samantha was lying.
- March 12 distribution date may affect damages/avoidability but does not erase earlier reliance.

Defense analysis:
- Accept half-truth doctrine in principle; focus on missing factual bridge. Plaintiff still must prove that the false reading premise made the completion timing assurance misleading as to completion by the deadline.
- Confession remains limited: not read full essay before first draft; implied she had; not downloaded complete text when presenting early conclusions. No admission that she had not read by March 11 evening, could not complete by then, missed handoff, or knew timing assurance was false when made.
- Session summary includes ongoing revision/preparation; this undercuts an automatic impossibility inference.
- Reasonableness/causation remains contested: Peter approving an $85,000 print run before final package is a commercial risk; approval note is internal; no cancellation terms or vendor irrevocability proof; March 12 distribution order postdates handoff and creates a real causation/mitigation gap.

No outside search performed: rebuttal introduced no external factual source; missing decisive materials are private case artifacts (handoff/draft/vendor terms). Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Plaintiff closing notes for arb-ex01-20260629175341.

Opportunity: closings:plaintiff. Final action allowed: deliver_closing_statement. No evidence submission or technical reports allowed.

Record scan: get_case/list_evidence show no new evidence after rebuttal/surrebuttal. Defense surrebuttal added only DX-S1 confession, DX-S2 session summary, DX-S3 distribution order, all from the same visible packet evidence. No new metadata changes or submitted evidence. Existing technical report remains plaintiff signature verification report.

Defense final position: false reading premise does not automatically prove false completion timing; confession does not say Samantha missed handoff or knew completion was impossible; session summary says later revision/preparation; reliance/cost causation are underproved due missing vendor cancellation terms and March 12 distribution order.

Closing plan: Focus on proposition, not damages. Burden is preponderance, not final handoff forensics. Decisive chain is: Peter gives explicit go/no-go production question; Samantha answers with false present-tense predicate (read Stephenson) and completion assurance; Peter relies and preserves/approves rush production; Samantha reconfirms before invoice; signed confession proves the predicate false and deceptive. Completion meant the assigned source-grounded package, not any words by deadline. Defense's 'could have corrected later' is only a possibility and no record evidence shows correction before handoff. Reasonable reliance shown because Peter asked directly after disclosing consequence; vendor cancellation terms affect damages/amount more than proposition. Acknowledge gaps: no final handoff/version record, session summary derivative, March 12 creates downstream cost questions; argue those do not defeat material misrepresentation and reliance.

Defendant closing work notes for arb-ex01-20260629175341.

Opportunity: closings:defendant. Final allowed action: deliver_closing_statement. No evidence admission or technical reports in closing.

Final record scan: list_evidence performed; no new submitted evidence files or changed packet items. Plaintiff closing filed. Exact reads this opportunity: confession, deadline-message-thread, distribution-work-order, session-summary. Prior exact reads covered approval note, invoice, instructions, signature/public key, damages/time-token log.

Closing evidence analysis:
- Plaintiff's strongest evidence remains the direct message thread: Peter framed a go/no-go deadline, Samantha said she had read Stephenson and could deliver in time, Peter said he would keep the rush plan, and Samantha reaffirmed the next morning.
- Authenticated confession is adverse but limited: not read full essay before first draft; implied she had; had not downloaded complete text when presenting early conclusions. It does not say missed handoff, unable to complete by handoff, source still unread by handoff, or knowingly false timing assurance.
- Session summary is derivative from Peter's notes and includes ongoing revision/preparation; it helps plaintiff on sequence but not the missing final handoff/draft state.
- Print approval note is internal subjective reliance evidence; no exact closing read of approval note this turn but read in prior opportunities. It does not show vendor cancellation/irrevocability or objective reasonableness.
- Distribution order is dated March 12 after promised handoff; it remains a causation/mitigation problem.

Closing strategy: acknowledge discomfort and reading-related misrepresentation; stress proposition-specific burden. Plaintiff proved assurance and a false reading premise before early drafts, not that the timing promise was materially false or that Peter reasonably relied in incurring irreversible costs. Missing natural evidence (final handoff, draft history, original notes, cancellation terms, post-handoff communications) should count against claimant at preponderance because these are the records that would answer the exact proposition.

Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.
